serviceAreaOrLocation: Kansas City regional area (27-county area in NW Missouri and NE Kansas)
serviceType: Regional Food Bank and Mobile Distribution
recentDemandChange: Demand doubled overnight during SNAP suspension; food insecurity at a 10-year high (1 in 7 individuals).
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Lost 3 million lbs of food in 2025 due to $500 million national USDA commodity cut; 41,000 cases of staples canceled.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Private charity; Hunt Family Foundation; Cable Dahmer Automotive Group; $500,000 from recent donors; government commodity programs.
operationalStrategy: Central hub supplying 600–900+ nonprofit agencies; provides 1 meal for every 9 provided by SNAP; hosts voter registration at sites.

serviceAreaOrLocation: National (including Kansas City, Johnson County, KS, and North Texas)
serviceType: Last-mile food delivery for food banks and nonprofits
recentDemandChange: Powered over 8 million deliveries (135 million+ meals) since 2018; 800,000+ deliveries in specific regions.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Addresses transportation barriers for seniors/homebound; existing federal/state funding is difficult to access for last-mile logistics.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: $1 million in Project DASH Impact Grants (2025); at-cost or subsidized delivery services for partners.
operationalStrategy: Leverages DoorDash Drive logistics platform to deliver food bank boxes to homebound or transit-constrained residents.

serviceAreaOrLocation: Atlanta, GA (MARTA rail stations including West End)
recentDemandChange: Increased from 2,500/3,500 visitors in 2015 to 13,000+ in 2016; reached more residents than existing grocers.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Focus on food deserts; limited by seasonality and restricted operating hours; surplus donated to food banks.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: $500,000 USDA grant; SNAP vouchers; Georgia Fresh for Less (double SNAP) scheme; MARTA partnership.
operationalStrategy: Managed by Organix Matters; pop-up stands inside high-traffic rail stations; weekly markets May–December.

serviceType: Wholesale distribution and retail capacity building
recentDemandChange: Produce sales rose 172% after SNAP incentives; 1 million+ units sold since start of pandemic; collard sales doubled after training.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: 2024 cuts to local SNAP supplements; small stores historically unable to meet wholesaler minimums or refrigeration costs.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: USDA GusNIP grants ($500,000 to $890,000); DC Health; Patrick J. McGovern Foundation; Longer Tables Fund.
operationalStrategy: Mission-driven wholesaler providing free refrigeration/training to small retailers; SNAP Match incentives (spend $5, get $5).

serviceAreaOrLocation: 31st Street and Prospect Avenue, Kansas City, MO (64127)
recentDemandChange: Closed August 2025; impacted 12,400 people; increased demand for local community gardens.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Closed due to $5 million loss, infrastructure issues (sewer smell), and security/crime concerns.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: $29 million total taxpayer funding (bonds/loans); $17 million city investment; $750,000 cash subsidy (May 2025).
operationalStrategy: Brick-and-mortar retail; city-owned shopping center previously operated by nonprofit Community Builders.

recentDemandChange: 85% of surveyed customers experienced food insecurity; operates 5 days a week at 19 locations.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: High vehicle maintenance and capital costs for retrofitted buses.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: NINR funding; Market Bucks (SNAP incentives); Wilder Foundation; IndieGoGo campaign ($12,000+).
operationalStrategy: Retrofitted buses visit subsidized housing sites; prices 13–38% lower than regional supermarkets via bulk wholesale partnerships.

recentDemandChange: Not in source
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Addresses distributor gap for 1.2 million people living more than 0.5 miles from fresh food; wholesalers often refuse small orders.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Consignment basis with 50 community partners.
operationalStrategy: Places coolers and baskets of fresh produce in 100+ independent corner stores in low-food-access tracts.

organizationOrProgramName: Community Action Council of Howard County (CAC)
serviceAreaOrLocation: Howard County, MD (Columbia, Laurel, Elkridge, Savage)
serviceType: Food Bank and Guaranteed Basic Income (GBI) Pilot
recentDemandChange: Identified 292 potential GBI participants; pilot currently serves 20 families.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Families struggling despite employment; 300,000+ people nationally lack access in transport areas.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: $750,000 nonprofit grant in County FY2025 Budget; GBI provides $1,000/month for 12 months.
operationalStrategy: Unrestricted financial support combined with wrap-around services; targeted at 150-300% of Federal Poverty Limits.

recentDemandChange: Record 131,968 rescues in 2024; nearly 32 million meals delivered.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Government cuts to food assistance increasing need for free distribution.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: 99.1% of donations go to program services; model turns $1 into 16 meals.
operationalStrategy: Tech-enabled model connecting surplus food from donors to hunger relief agencies via volunteers.

recentDemandChange: 10% increase per month; record month and biggest distribution day ever recorded.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Commodities decreased from 6 pallets to 3; no longer receiving animal proteins.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: TEFAP (The Emergency Food Assistance Program) and Section 32 (bonus TEFAP).
operationalStrategy: Fixed pantry location managing exponential growth in community need.

recentDemandChange: Daily client count increased from 10–15 to approximately 40 people.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Struggling to keep food on shelves due to surge of clients during government shutdown.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Donations and community food drives.
operationalStrategy: Pantry coordination and community food drives.

recentDemandChange: Distributed 2,300 lbs of food to 300+ individuals in first week of SNAP disruption.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Shelves stripped due to demand surge and federal commodity cuts.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Not in source
operationalStrategy: Car line distribution and emergency food kit assembly.

recentDemandChange: 50 household slots filled in a matter of hours.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Currently in pilot phase.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Partnership between KC Black Urban Growers, urban farmers, and KC Defender.
operationalStrategy: Stopgap community solution following Sun Fresh closure.

recentDemandChange: Uptick in neighbors needing services; goal to give away 2,000 lbs of food in 2025.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Distributing produce as a bandage for grocery closures.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Nonprofit organization.
operationalStrategy: Managing community gardens (e.g., Ruby Jeans) and distributing fresh produce for free to elderly/low-income neighbors.

recentDemandChange: Area returning to food desert status at end of 2025.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Closing due to poor financial performance and lack of nearby residential density/foot traffic.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: $7 million investment from Unified Government of Wyandotte County and KCK.
operationalStrategy: Cooperative grocery model based on Lawrence, KS parent store.

recentDemandChange: Served nearly 344,000 New Yorkers last year.
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Market hours adjusted due to weather conditions.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: Donations; community partnerships.
operationalStrategy: Outdoor farmers' market-style distributions; includes cooking demonstrations and nutrition education.

recentDemandChange: Not in source
supplyStatusOrChallenges: Distribution gap between small stores and large wholesalers; fresh produce suppliers lack free delivery options.
keyFundingOrSupportStreams: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NIH award R34HL145368).
operationalStrategy: Multilevel systems intervention (BuddyUp purchasing and BuddyLift shared delivery) to reduce sourcing costs.
